question: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

baseline.answer:
Excalidraw uses an imperative canvas renderer wrapped in React, with the scene and the UI overlay split across separate canvases.

1. The scene is the source of truth. When elements change, `Scene.replaceAllElements()` or `Scene.mutateElement()` calls `triggerUpdate()`, which bumps a `sceneNonce` and notifies listeners. See [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L303) and [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L441).
2. `App` subscribes to scene updates with `this.scene.onUpdate(this.triggerRender)`, so a scene change causes a React rerender. `triggerRender()` either does a normal `setState({})` rerender or forces a scene update. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L3235) and [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L4800).
3. During render, `App` asks `Renderer.getRenderableElements()` for the current drawable set. That method memoizes the result, filters out edited text and the in-progress element when needed, computes visible elements, and builds `canvasNonce` from the scene nonce plus the new element version nonce. See [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L159) and [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L210).
4. `StaticCanvas` and `InteractiveCanvas` are both `React.memo(...)` components with custom equality checks. When their relevant props change, they run a `useEffect` that imperatively redraws the canvas. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L33) and [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L86).
5. `StaticCanvas` renders the actual scene: it sizes the canvas, bootstraps the 2D context, clears/fills the background, draws the grid, then paints visible elements with `renderElement()`. It also handles frame clipping, bound text, link icons, and pending flowchart nodes. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L59) and [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L236).
6. `InteractiveCanvas` renders the UI overlay on top of the scene: selection boxes, transform handles, snap lines, remote cursors, scrollbars, and animation-driven binding highlights. It uses `AnimationController` to keep the overlay animating when needed. See [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L173) and [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L1550).
7. While you are creating or dragging a new shape, Excalidraw can render just that in a separate `NewElementCanvas` overlay instead of repainting the full scene. See [NewElementCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/NewElementCanvas.tsx#L25) and [renderNewElementScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderNewElementScene.ts#L16).
8. `App` passes `canvasNonce` and `selectionNonce` into both canvas components so React knows when to redraw even if the underlying arrays/maps are structurally stable. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2381) and [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2431).

In short: state changes invalidate the `Scene`, `App` rerenders, the `Renderer` computes the drawable subset and nonces, and the memoized canvas components imperatively repaint only when those derived inputs change.

If you want, I can also trace one specific flow end-to-end, like “draw a rectangle”, “drag selection”, or “remote collaborator update”.
